summary: Joe Reis argues that data modeling is critically endangered in the industry, and without reviving and modernizing this practice, companies rushing to implement AI and LLMs will fail because their underlying data is fundamentally broken. He proposes specific reforms including making data modeling a core practice across all teams, ending religious wars between modeling methodologies, and adopting a data product mindset.
key_points: Most companies' underlying data is in shambles, making AI initiatives built on top of it destined to fail
Semantic layers and data catalogs are bandaids that don't solve the root problem of poor upstream data
Data modeling must become a core practice across software, applications, analytics, data engineering, and ML teams
The industry needs to stop petty religious wars between data modeling methodologies
A data product mindset is essential as data use extends beyond reports and dashboards
money_quotes: Most companies are still barely doing BI, and now they want to do AI. This won't work.
A semantic layer is, at best, a bandaid that doesn't solve the core problem that a lot of upstream data you depend upon is built on incredibly shaky ground these days.

To all the purists out there who've been arguing for decades that your way is the one true way of data modeling - your tirades and idiotic behavior are holding the industry back. If your approach had worked by now, we wouldn't be in this mess.
If we can't get data modeling right, we're in for a world of hurt.
key_insight: The AI gold rush will collapse unless the industry returns to data modeling fundamentals, because no amount of semantic layers or tooling can fix the broken data foundations most companies are building on.
